articles.title: Police arrest 22-year-old, recover firearms, suspected Indian hemp in Lagos
articles.description: By Esther Onyegbula Operatives of the Lagos State Police Command have arrested a 22-year-old suspect, Olamide Suleiman, during a stop-and-search operation in the Aguda area of the state, recovering firearms and substances suspected to be Indian hemp. The comm…

articles.title: Police arrest 22-year-old, recover firearms, suspected Indian hemp in Lagos
articles.description:
According to the Police Public Relations Officer, SP Abimbola Adebisi, a search conducted on the suspect led to the recovery of dry leaves suspected to be Indian hemp.
The post Police arrest 22-year-old, recover firearms, suspected Indian hemp in Lagos appear…

articles.title: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafari bid for religious freedom marijuana exemption
articles.description: Kenya's small Rastafarian community was seeking legal permission to smoke marijuana under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The judge found they did not show it to be a necessary part of their worship. A Kenyan court on Wednesday refused to gra…

articles.title: Kenyan court dismisses Rastafarians' bid to legalise cannabis
articles.description: The court acknowledged the need for a broader national debate on cannabis policy in Kenya.

articles.title: NDLEA arrests S’African woman over 5.75kg cannabis haul
articles.description:
NDLEA arrests a South African woman attempting to smuggle heroin at Abuja airport, seizes 5.75kg cannabis, and intercepts other drug hauls.

Read More: https://punchng.com/ndlea-arrests-safrican-woman-over-5-75kg-cannabis-haul/

articles.title: Smoking Out Virginia’s Pot Law
articles.description: If you are unclear about Virginia’s marijuana laws, rest assured you are not alone these days. Even police officers seem confused (if not necessarily dazed). “As of July 1, 2026, there are no Code of Virginia violations related to marijuana,” a state police l…

articles.title: Cannabis Law Reform Is a Civil Rights Issue and Must Be Treated as Such
articles.description:
NORML Summer ‘26 Mitchell Floch shares his perspective on the disparate impact of marijuana prohibition.
The post Cannabis Law Reform Is a Civil Rights Issue and Must Be Treated as Such appeared first on NORML.

articles.title: NDLEA seizes N12.4bn cannabis at Lagos Port, busts drug syndicate targeting passengers
articles.description:
Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have intercepted a 40-foot container loaded with 4,959 kilograms of Canadian Loud, a potent strain of cannabis, valued at N12.397 billion in street value at the Apapa Port in Lagos.
The post NDLEA…

articles.title: NDLEA busts drug syndicate, seizes ₦12.3bn Canadian cannabis at Lagos port
articles.description:
NDLEA intercepts 4,959kg of Canadian Loud cannabis worth ₦12.3bn at Lagos port, dismantling a drug syndicate and making multiple arrests.

Read More: https://punchng.com/ndlea-busts-drug-syndicate-seizes-%e2%82%a612-3bn-canadian-cannabis-at-lagos-port/

articles.title: NDLEA destroys 51 hectares of cannabis, arrests 281 suspects in Ekiti
articles.description:
NDLEA Ekiti has destroyed over 51 hectares of cannabis plantations and arrested 281 suspects in the past year, combating drug trafficking and abuse in the

articles.title: C’River: NDLEA arrests 262 drug offenders in seven months, destroys cannabis farms
articles.description:
The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), Cross River State Command, says it arrested 262 suspected drug offenders between November 2025 and May 2026 as part of intensified efforts against narcotics distribution in the state.

articles.title: Vermont: Governor Signs Bill Into Law Raising Adult-Use Marijuana Possession Limits
articles.description:
Vermont is the third state this year to significantly increase adult-use marijuana possession limits.
The post Vermont: Governor Signs Bill Into Law Raising Adult-Use Marijuana Possession Limits appeared first on NORML.

articles.title: Amicus With Dahlia Lithwick | Law, justice, and the courts
articles.description: The Supreme Court handed down a unanimous ruling this week in United States v. Hemani, holding that a marijuana user cannot be stripped of his Second Amendment right to own a firearm simply because he sometimes uses cannabis. Justice Neil Gorsuch wrote the ma…

articles.title: Second Amendment Advocates Cheer Supreme Court Ruling Striking Down Law Barring Marijuana Users from Having Guns
articles.description:
The Supreme Court issued an opinion striking down a federal law barring "unlawful users" of marijuana from having guns, but noted the ruling was a "narrow" one.
The post Second Amendment Advocates Cheer Supreme Court Ruling Striking Down Law Barring Marijuana…

articles.title: Supreme Court sides with a Texas man who says it’s not a crime for marijuana users to have guns
articles.description: The decision is a loss for President Trump’s Republican administration, which had defended the 1968 law despite arguing against other gun restrictions.

articles.title: Supreme Court Sides With Texas Man Who Says It’s Not A Crime For Marijuana Users To Have Guns
articles.description: The justices unanimously agreed that a law barring guns from anyone who uses drugs illegally violates the Second Amendment.

articles.title: Supreme Court sides with marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
articles.description: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.

articles.title: Supreme Court sides with marijuana user who wants to legally own a gun
articles.description: The Supreme Court is siding with a Texas marijuana user who wants to legally own a gun, the latest in a line of firearm cases from a court that has expanded gun rights. The unanimous decision was handed down Thursday in the case of Ali Danial Hemani, who argu…

articles.title: Supreme Court backs a marijuana user’s challenge to a restriction on gun ownership
articles.description: The Supreme Court endorsed a marijuana user's challenge to a law that bars people who consume illegal drugs from having firearms, the same law that Hunter Biden was convicted under.

articles.title: Customs, NDLEA intercept N13bn cannabis, expired drugs

milestones.summary: DEA began formal proceedings on the proposal to move marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III; the hearing does not itself complete rescheduling.
Draft regulations propose possession, cultivation and transport limits under the Cannabis for Private Purposes Act.

milestones.status: Cannabis for Private Purposes Act signed
milestones.summary: The Act regulates adult private possession and use, prohibits dealing, and provides for expungement of specified convictions; commencement is by proclamation.
Germany permitted limited adult possession, private cultivation and non-commercial cultivation associations under the Cannabis Act.
